design_summary.name: RV32EC_Zmmul CPU
design_summary.description: Compact architectural phase plan for an RV32EC_Zmmul core with fetch, decode, execute/writeback, RV32E register file, CSR/trap handling, load/store handshake, compressed instruction support, multiply support, and SPI boot assist integration.

modules.name: compressed_decoder
modules.description: Expands supported RV32C/RV32EC compressed instructions into internal 32-bit form and flags illegal compressed encodings.

behavior_contract.reset_behavior: No reset port. Outputs are pure combinational functions of c_instr_i.
behavior_contract.handshake_protocol: None. Input c_instr_i may change every cycle; outputs must reflect the decoded result without requiring enable/valid.
behavior_contract.output_timing: expanded_instr_o, is_compressed_o, and illegal_o must settle combinationally from c_instr_i in the same cycle.
behavior_contract.retention_rules: No internal retention. Unsupported encodings must drive illegal_o=1 and expanded_instr_o to a deterministic value chosen by implementation, while supported compressed encodings drive is_compressed_o=1.
behavior_contract.completion_semantics: A supported compressed instruction completes decode when is_compressed_o=1 and illegal_o=0. Illegal compressed patterns complete with illegal_o=1.
forbidden_assumptions: Do not assume all 16-bit patterns are valid compressed instructions.
Do not assume expanded_instr_o is meaningful when illegal_o=1 unless explicitly defined.
Do not assume any sequential storage or stall interaction.

modules.name: immediate_generator
modules.description: Generates expanded immediates for base and compressed decode paths.

behavior_contract.reset_behavior: No reset port. imm_o is a pure combinational function of instr_i and imm_sel_i.
behavior_contract.handshake_protocol: None. Immediate output is continuously decoded from current inputs.
behavior_contract.output_timing: imm_o must settle combinationally in the same cycle after instr_i or imm_sel_i changes.
behavior_contract.retention_rules: No internal state. Unsupported imm_sel_i values should produce a deterministic implementation-defined imm_o, preferably zero.
behavior_contract.completion_semantics: Immediate generation is complete in the same cycle as the input presentation.
forbidden_assumptions: Do not assume only base RV32I immediates are needed; compressed-expanded instructions may also use normal formats.
Do not assume zero extension except where ISA requires it.
Do not assume state retention between evaluations.

modules.name: decode_control_unit
modules.description: Decodes RV32E/C/Zmmul instructions, classifies operation type, and flags unsupported or illegal encodings including x16-x31 usage.

signals.name: instr_i
signals.description: Instruction word after decompression if needed

behavior_contract.reset_behavior: No reset port. All outputs are derived combinationally from instr_i and instr_is_compressed_i.
behavior_contract.handshake_protocol: None. Module decodes one instruction word at a time without request/acknowledge.
behavior_contract.output_timing: All outputs must settle combinationally in the same cycle after inputs change.
behavior_contract.retention_rules: No state retention. For illegal instructions, illegal_o must assert and all nonessential control outputs should be driven to deterministic benign defaults.
behavior_contract.completion_semantics: Decode is complete when control outputs and register indices reflect the current instruction. Illegal RV32E register references x16..x31, unsupported divide/rem, and unsupported extensions must assert illegal_o.
forbidden_assumptions: Do not assume x16..x31 can be silently remapped or ignored.
Do not assume divide/remainder instructions are partially supported.
Do not assume compressed source means the 32-bit expanded instruction can violate RV32E legality.

modules.name: alu32
modules.description: 32-bit integer ALU for arithmetic, logical, shift, and compare operations.

behavior_contract.reset_behavior: No reset port. result_o is purely combinational.
behavior_contract.output_timing: result_o must settle combinationally from a_i, b_i, and alu_op_i in the same cycle.
behavior_contract.retention_rules: No state retention. Unsupported alu_op_i values should produce a deterministic implementation-defined result, preferably zero.
behavior_contract.completion_semantics: Operation completes in the same cycle as input presentation.
forbidden_assumptions: Do not assume signedness is the same for SLT and SLTU.
Do not assume shift amount uses all 32 bits; RTL should use architecturally relevant low bits.
Do not assume sequential behavior.

modules.name: branch_jump_unit
modules.description: Evaluates branch conditions and computes branch, JAL, and JALR redirect targets.

behavior_contract.reset_behavior: No reset port. Outputs are combinational from current inputs.
behavior_contract.handshake_protocol: None. Branch/jump decision is evaluated from a single current instruction context.
behavior_contract.output_timing: take_o, target_o, and link_addr_o must settle combinationally in the same cycle after inputs change.
behavior_contract.retention_rules: No retained state. If no control transfer is active, take_o must be 0 and target_o may be a deterministic don't-care-safe value.
behavior_contract.completion_semantics: For branch instructions, take_o depends on branch_funct3_i and rs comparisons. For JAL/JALR, take_o asserts unconditionally. JALR target_o must clear bit 0.
forbidden_assumptions: Do not assume link_addr_o can infer instruction length internally from unavailable metadata.
Do not assume JALR preserves target bit 0.
Do not assume branch_funct3_i values outside supported branch encodings are valid.

modules.name: multiplier_unit
modules.description: Multi-cycle Zmmul multiply engine with start/busy/done handshake.

behavior_contract.reset_behavior: Active-low asynchronous reset clears busy_o, done_o, and any internal product/state. After rst_n=0, outputs must be idle.
state_elements: latched operand/state for multiply in progress
behavior_contract.handshake_protocol: A multiply transaction begins when start_i is asserted while idle. busy_o asserts during the operation. done_o pulses for one cycle when result_o is valid. New starts while busy are ignored or not accepted.
behavior_contract.output_timing: busy_o changes on clock edges. done_o is a registered pulse. result_o is valid on the done cycle and may remain stable until next accepted start.
behavior_contract.retention_rules: Operands and partial result are retained while busy_o=1. result_o retains last completed result until next completion or reset.
behavior_contract.completion_semantics: Exactly one done_o pulse is produced per accepted start_i. Completion latency is implementation-dependent but finite and at least one clocked step.
forbidden_assumptions: Do not assume single-cycle multiply.
Do not assume a new start can preempt a busy operation.
Do not assume done_o stays high beyond one completion pulse.

modules.name: register_file_rv32e
modules.description: 16x32 integer register file with x0 hardwired to zero and two read ports plus one write port.

behavior_contract.reset_behavior: No reset port. Architectural x0 is always zero. Other registers power up unspecified unless separately initialized by software or implementation policy.
behavior_contract.handshake_protocol: Synchronous single write port sampled on clock edge when we_i=1. Two read ports are continuously addressable.
behavior_contract.output_timing: rdata1_o and rdata2_o reflect addressed register contents combinationally from stored state. Writes take effect after the active clock edge.
behavior_contract.retention_rules: x1..x15 retain prior values until overwritten. Writes to x0 are ignored and x0 always reads as zero.
behavior_contract.completion_semantics: A write is complete after the clock edge that samples we_i=1 and legal waddr_i!=0.
forbidden_assumptions: Do not assume reset clears x1..x15.
Do not assume x0 can be written.
Do not assume read-during-write bypass unless explicitly implemented.

modules.name: csr_trap_controller
modules.description: Machine-mode CSR block with trap entry, interrupt handling, mepc/mcause capture, mtvec redirect, and MRET return control.

behavior_contract.reset_behavior: Active-low asynchronous reset initializes machine CSR state to defined reset values, clears trap_taken_o and mret_redirect_o pulses, and initializes mtvec/mepc/mcause according to implementation defaults.
state_elements: optional mstatus bits needed for simple interrupt enable/return semantics
behavior_contract.handshake_protocol: Trap event inputs are sampled on the clock. trap_taken_o pulses for one cycle on accepted trap entry. mret_i causes a one-cycle mret_redirect_o pulse. CSR writes occur on clock edge when csr_we_i=1.
behavior_contract.output_timing: trap_taken_o and mret_redirect_o are registered pulses. trap_pc_o reflects current mtvec when trap_taken_o is asserted. csr_rdata_o is combinational or registered from current CSR state but must be deterministic and stable within the cycle.
behavior_contract.retention_rules: mepc and mcause retain saved values until overwritten by a later trap or CSR write if writable. mtvec retains programmed value until reset or CSR write.
behavior_contract.completion_semantics: Illegal instruction, ebreak, and external interrupt events create trap entry and save mepc/mcause. MRET redirects to saved mepc without generating trap_taken_o.
forbidden_assumptions: Do not assume vectored mtvec mode support.
Do not assume nested interrupt preemption.
Do not assume ext_irq_i is level-sensitive forever accepted without enable/state checks beyond implemented machine-mode policy.

modules.name: load_store_unit
modules.description: Address generation and memory handshake block for variable-latency loads/stores with byte-enable generation.

behavior_contract.reset_behavior: Active-low asynchronous reset clears pending transaction state, deasserts mem_req_o, clears busy_o, and clears any retained load result/output registers.
state_elements: latched store data and byte enables
behavior_contract.handshake_protocol: A transaction starts when req_valid_i=1 while idle. mem_req_o remains asserted until accepted/serviced according to implementation. For stores, completion may occur on grant. For loads, busy_o remains high until mem_rvalid_i=1 returns data.
behavior_contract.output_timing: Request outputs are driven from current or latched transaction context. load_data_o becomes valid on or after the cycle mem_rvalid_i=1 is observed. busy_o is registered and reflects outstanding work.
behavior_contract.retention_rules: While busy_o=1, address/control/data for the active transaction must remain stable. load_data_o retains last completed load value until next load completion or reset.
behavior_contract.completion_semantics: Store completes after request acceptance under implementation policy. Load completes when response valid arrives and busy_o deasserts.
forbidden_assumptions: Do not assume zero wait-state memory.
Do not assume mem_rvalid_i arrives in same cycle as grant.
Do not assume misalignment trapping exists in this block unless explicitly implemented elsewhere.

modules.name: boot_rom_loader_assist
modules.description: SPI boot assist FSM that validates image header, copies image into IRAM, and reports boot completion or terminal fail-stop.

behavior_contract.reset_behavior: Active-low asynchronous reset clears boot FSM to idle, deasserts spi_tx_req_o and iram_we_o, clears boot_done_o, and clears fail_stop_o.
behavior_contract.handshake_protocol: When start_i is asserted, the module requests SPI words via spi_tx_req_o. Header words are consumed when spi_rx_valid_i=1. Image copy words generate one-cycle IRAM writes. Any spi_error_i or validation failure drives terminal fail_stop_o.
behavior_contract.output_timing: spi_tx_req_o is driven by FSM state. iram_we_o pulses when a valid image word is accepted for copy. boot_done_o and fail_stop_o are registered status outputs.
behavior_contract.retention_rules: Once fail_stop_o asserts, it remains asserted until reset. Once boot_done_o asserts, it remains asserted until reset unless implementation explicitly returns idle while preserving done status.
behavior_contract.completion_semantics: Boot completes successfully only after a valid header and exactly the declared image payload have been received and written. Failure is terminal and suppresses boot_done_o.
forbidden_assumptions: Do not assume all SPI input words are valid without spi_rx_valid_i.
Do not assume malformed header can be recovered without reset.
Do not assume image size or start address outside allowed range is acceptable.

modules.name: fetch_unit
modules.description: Front-end PC and fetch request generator with redirect priority for trap, MRET, branch/jump, and sequential compressed-aware stepping.

behavior_contract.reset_behavior: Active-low asynchronous reset loads PC from reset_vector_i upon release and initializes fetch outputs to request from that address.
behavior_contract.handshake_protocol: instr_req_o is continuously asserted in normal run mode. PC advances on each clock when not stalled, unless redirected. Redirect priority is trap over MRET over branch/jump over sequential increment.
behavior_contract.output_timing: pc_o and instr_addr_o are driven from the registered PC and update on clock edges. instr_req_o is level-based and normally asserted whenever fetch is active.
behavior_contract.retention_rules: When stall_i=1 and no higher-priority redirect is accepted by implementation policy, PC holds. flush_i clears younger pipeline contents externally but does not by itself require a new PC unless combined with redirect.
behavior_contract.completion_semantics: Sequential completion uses instr_is_16b_i to choose PC+2 versus PC+4. Redirects replace sequential advance in the update cycle.
forbidden_assumptions: Do not assume branch prediction.
Do not assume flush_i alone implies a redirect target.
Do not assume instruction length can be inferred from fetched data inside this module without instr_is_16b_i.
Do not require proof of hidden regfile contents, hidden ALU result buses, or hidden CSR state at top level unless exposed by a port.
Treat datapath-class top-level tests as observable integration flows unless the final value is visible at the top interface.

modules.name: execute_writeback_unit
modules.description: Execute-stage result selection and writeback control coordinating ALU, multiply, load, and CSR result paths and stall requests.

behavior_contract.reset_behavior: Active-low asynchronous reset clears pending execution state and deasserts wb_we_o, mul_start_o, and stall_req_o.
state_elements: registered writeback control/data outputs if implemented
behavior_contract.handshake_protocol: ALU, load, and CSR writeback may complete immediately for the current context. Multiply uses start/done handshake: mul_start_o pulses to launch, stall_req_o remains asserted while waiting, and writeback occurs when mul_done_i=1.
behavior_contract.output_timing: For immediate-completion operations, wb outputs may be combinational-from-input-context or registered for next cycle, but test contract samples after one clocked step. mul_start_o and stall_req_o are generated in response to current context. wb outputs are valid on completion cycle.
behavior_contract.retention_rules: During a multiply wait, destination context must be retained until mul_done_i. wb outputs should not spuriously reassert for the same transaction after completion.
behavior_contract.completion_semantics: Exactly one writeback is generated per completed ALU/load/CSR/MUL instruction when rd_i targets an architectural destination and operation semantics require writeback.
forbidden_assumptions: Do not assume multiply is single-cycle.
Do not assume load_data_i is valid unless load transaction has completed externally.
Do not assume every instruction class writes back.
Do not require proof of hidden regfile contents, hidden ALU result buses, or hidden CSR state at top level unless exposed by a port.
Treat datapath-class top-level tests as observable integration flows unless the final value is visible at the top interface.

modules.name: rv32ec_zmmul_cpu_top
modules.description: Top-level 3-stage CPU integration including boot flow, fetch/decode/execute composition, register file, CSR/trap path, memory interfaces, and observable integration outputs.

behavior_contract.reset_behavior: Active-low asynchronous reset clears pipeline valid state, cancels outstanding observable transactions under implementation policy, deasserts boot_done and fail_stop, initializes trap_taken low, and starts fetch from reset vector or boot ROM policy after release.
behavior_contract.handshake_protocol: Instruction fetch uses instr_req/instr_addr with external instr_rvalid response. Data access uses data_req/data_we/data_be/data_addr/data_wdata with external data_gnt/data_rvalid/data_rdata. SPI boot uses spi_tx_req with spi_rx_valid/spi_rx_data/spi_error. In fail-stop state, externally visible requests should quiesce except where implementation intentionally holds safe idle levels.
behavior_contract.output_timing: Observable outputs update on clock boundaries in response to accepted fetches, redirects, memory handshakes, traps, and boot FSM progress. trap_taken is a pulse-like observable event; trap_pc is valid when trap_taken is asserted.
behavior_contract.retention_rules: boot_done remains asserted after successful boot until reset. fail_stop remains asserted until reset. During stalled multiply or load waits, visible request/redirect behavior must remain consistent with held pipeline context.
behavior_contract.completion_semantics: Integration behavior is judged only by top-level visible outputs and handshakes: fetch progression, branch/jump redirects, load/store requests, trap entry/return visibility, SPI boot completion/failure, and fail-stop behavior.
forbidden_assumptions: Do not assume internal pipeline signals are observable to the testbench.
Do not assume unsupported instructions produce any visible effect other than legal trap behavior.
Do not assume CPU executes while SPI boot assist owns startup flow in boot_mode_spi.
Do not require proof of hidden regfile contents, hidden ALU result buses, or hidden CSR state at top level unless exposed by a port.
Treat datapath-class top-level tests as observable integration flows unless the final value is visible at the top interface.

external_interfaces.name: instruction_fetch_if
external_interfaces.description: Top-level observable instruction fetch request/response handshake for integration verification

external_interfaces.name: data_memory_if
external_interfaces.description: Top-level observable load/store request and response handshake for integration verification

external_interfaces.name: spi_boot_if
external_interfaces.description: Top-level SPI boot assist observable request and receive signaling

external_interfaces.name: trap_observation_if
external_interfaces.description: Top-level observable trap event and redirect PC outputs

testcases.stimulus: clk toggling; rst_n=0 for at least 1 cycle then rst_n=1; boot_mode_spi=0; ext_irq=0; instr_rvalid=0; instr_rdata=0x00000000; data_gnt=0; data_rvalid=0; data_rdata=0x00000000; spi_rx_valid=0; spi_rx_data=0x00000000; spi_error=0.
testcases.expected: Core exits reset into normal fetch mode. instr_req asserts and instr_addr equals reset vector. boot_done remains 0, fail_stop remains 0.

checks.condition: After reset release in non-SPI boot mode
First observable fetch after reset release

timing_contract.notes: Sample one clock after rst_n rises to allow registered fetch startup.

testcases.stimulus: rst_n=1; boot_mode_spi=0; ext_irq=0; no redirects or traps injected; provide instr_rvalid=1 with instr_rdata=0x00000001 so low bits[1:0]!=2'b11 indicating a 16-bit compressed instruction in low halfword; keep data_gnt=0 data_rvalid=0 spi inputs idle.
testcases.expected: Next observable fetch address advances by 2 bytes. No trap and no fail-stop.

checks.condition: On the fetch following the compressed instruction acceptance

timing_contract.notes: Sample on cycle after instr_rvalid compressed response is presented.

testcases.stimulus: rst_n=1; boot_mode_spi=0; ext_irq=0; provide instr_rvalid=1 with instr_rdata=0x00000033 so low bits[1:0]=2'b11 indicating a 32-bit instruction; other inputs idle and no redirects/traps.
testcases.expected: Next observable fetch address advances by 4 bytes. No trap and no fail-stop.

checks.condition: On the fetch following the 32-bit instruction acceptance

timing_contract.notes: Observe next fetch address after uncompressed instruction response.

testcases.stimulus: rst_n=1; boot_mode_spi=0; feed a valid instruction stream such that a taken branch executes, with instr_rvalid asserted on needed fetch responses and any needed register setup provided by preceding instructions; ext_irq=0; memory and SPI idle unless required by the setup program.
testcases.expected: Top-level next visible instruction fetch redirects to the branch target. No trap and no fail-stop.

checks.condition: On first fetch after branch resolution

timing_contract.notes: Sample first fetch address after execute-stage branch resolution becomes architecturally visible.

testcases.stimulus: rst_n=1; boot_mode_spi=0; supply a valid JAL instruction via instr_rvalid/instr_rdata with target offset encoded, plus any sequential responses needed; ext_irq=0; other interfaces idle.
testcases.expected: Observable fetch redirects to jal_target. No trap and no fail-stop.

checks.condition: On first fetch after JAL execution

timing_contract.notes: Check visible redirect only; internal link writeback is not top-level observable.

testcases.stimulus: rst_n=1; boot_mode_spi=0; execute a setup sequence to load rs1 with a known value, then present a valid JALR instruction with instr_rvalid responses; ext_irq=0; other interfaces idle.
testcases.expected: Observable fetch redirects to jalr_target_aligned with bit 0 cleared. No trap and no fail-stop.

checks.condition: On first fetch after JALR execution

timing_contract.notes: Observe aligned redirect address only.

testcases.stimulus: rst_n=1; boot_mode_spi=0; execute a valid load instruction sequence producing a known effective address; drive data_gnt=1 when data_req appears; later drive data_rvalid=1 and data_rdata=0x12345678; ext_irq=0; instr interface supplies legal instructions.
testcases.expected: A visible load request occurs with data_req=1, data_we=0, and data_addr=effective_address. No fail-stop and no unexpected trap.

checks.condition: Throughout legal load sequence absent unrelated trap causes

timing_contract.notes: Check request phase visibility, not internal register writeback.

testcases.stimulus: rst_n=1; boot_mode_spi=0; execute a valid store instruction sequence producing a known effective address and store data; drive data_gnt=1 when request appears; ext_irq=0; instruction responses valid.
testcases.expected: Visible store request with data_req=1, data_we=1, data_addr=effective_address, data_wdata=store_data, and expected data_be. No unexpected trap or fail-stop.

checks.condition: Throughout legal store sequence absent unrelated trap causes

timing_contract.notes: Observe top-level data interface only.

testcases.stimulus: rst_n=1; boot_mode_spi=0; execute a valid load sequence; drive data_gnt=1 but hold data_rvalid=0 for several cycles before eventually responding; instr interface continues presenting whatever is requested; ext_irq=0.
testcases.expected: Pending load remains externally visible via persistent or pending data_req behavior, and no new completed external redirect/trap occurs until the response returns. fail_stop remains 0.

checks.condition: During load wait-state window before data_rvalid=1
During load wait-state window absent unrelated trap causes

timing_contract.notes: Checker should observe behavior over several cycles while data_rvalid remains low.

testcases.stimulus: rst_n=1; boot_mode_spi=0; execute a legal MUL instruction sequence with valid instruction flow and any needed operand setup; ext_irq=0; memory and SPI idle unless required by setup.
testcases.expected: No trap_taken and no fail_stop. Front-end fetch progression resumes after multiply latency.

checks.condition: After multiply completion when fetch progression resumes

timing_contract.notes: Observe only no-trap/no-fail and resumed top-level fetch activity after MUL completion.

testcases.stimulus: rst_n=1; boot_mode_spi=0; provide instr_rvalid=1 with a DIV encoding in instr_rdata or expanded stream such that decode sees unsupported divide; ext_irq=0; other interfaces idle.
testcases.expected: Illegal-instruction trap is taken; trap_pc equals mtvec; fail_stop remains 0.

checks.condition: On cycle trap entry becomes visible
When trap_taken=1 for DIV illegal instruction

timing_contract.notes: trap_taken is sampled as a pulse-like event.

testcases.stimulus: rst_n=1; boot_mode_spi=0; provide instr_rvalid=1 with an unsupported REM encoding; ext_irq=0; other interfaces idle.
testcases.expected: Illegal-instruction trap is taken; trap_pc equals mtvec; fail_stop remains 0.

checks.condition: On cycle trap entry becomes visible
When trap_taken=1 for REM illegal instruction

timing_contract.notes: Observe top-level trap pulse only.

testcases.stimulus: rst_n=1; boot_mode_spi=0; provide instr_rvalid=1 with an instruction encoding that names x16 in rd, rs1, or rs2; ext_irq=0; other interfaces idle.
testcases.expected: Illegal-instruction trap is taken; trap_pc equals mtvec; fail_stop remains 0.

checks.condition: On cycle trap entry becomes visible
When trap_taken=1 for illegal x16..x31 register use

timing_contract.notes: Test only externally visible trap behavior.

testcases.stimulus: rst_n=1; boot_mode_spi=0; provide instr_rvalid=1 with an unsupported extension opcode; ext_irq=0; other interfaces idle.
testcases.expected: Illegal-instruction trap is taken; trap_pc equals mtvec; fail_stop remains 0.

checks.condition: On cycle trap entry becomes visible

timing_contract.notes: Observe top-level trap reporting only.

testcases.stimulus: rst_n=1; boot_mode_spi=0; core is running legal instruction flow; assert ext_irq=1 in a context where traps are enabled; keep other interfaces legal.
testcases.expected: trap_taken asserts and trap_pc equals mtvec. fail_stop remains 0.

checks.condition: When trap_taken=1 on external interrupt entry

timing_contract.notes: Allow implementation-dependent entry latency; checker waits up to bounded timeout.

testcases.stimulus: rst_n=1; boot_mode_spi=0; first create a prior trap entry to save mepc, then execute a trap handler stream containing MRET with valid instruction responses; ext_irq deasserted during return phase.
testcases.expected: Observable fetch redirects to saved mepc after MRET. trap_taken deasserts after return flow and fail_stop remains 0.

checks.condition: On first fetch after MRET redirect

timing_contract.notes: Observe redirected fetch to mepc only.

testcases.stimulus: rst_n=1; boot_mode_spi=1; assert a valid spi_rx_valid sequence carrying legal boot header/version/program_size/start_addr followed by full image data; spi_error=0; instruction/data response inputs idle.
testcases.expected: spi_tx_req asserts during image fetch activity, boot_done asserts on successful completion, fail_stop remains 0.

checks.condition: After full valid image is received

timing_contract.notes: boot_done is sampled after final image word acceptance.

testcases.stimulus: rst_n=1; boot_mode_spi=1; present spi_rx_valid=1 with malformed header word/sequence on spi_rx_data; spi_error=0; other inputs idle.

timing_contract.notes: fail_stop is terminal until reset.

testcases.stimulus: rst_n=1; boot_mode_spi=1; present a valid initial header sequence except with program_size>=16384 or start address outside allowed range; spi_error=0; other inputs idle.

timing_contract.notes: Either size or start-address violation may trigger this failure path.

testcases.stimulus: rst_n=1; boot_mode_spi=1; assert spi_error=1 during any active boot transaction; spi_rx_valid and spi_rx_data arbitrary; instruction/data response inputs idle.

checks.condition: After SPI error is observed during boot
After SPI error is observed during boot

timing_contract.notes: Terminal fail-stop expected on SPI error.

testcases.stimulus: clk toggling; rst_n=0 then rst_n=1; stall_i=0; flush_i=0; trap_redirect_valid_i=0; trap_redirect_pc_i=0x00000000; mret_redirect_valid_i=0; mret_redirect_pc_i=0x00000000; branch_redirect_valid_i=0; branch_redirect_pc_i=0x00000000; instr_is_16b_i=0; reset_vector_i=0x00000100.
testcases.expected: After reset release, pc_o=0x00000100, instr_req_o=1, instr_addr_o=0x00000100.

timing_contract.notes: Sample after reset deassertion edge.

testcases.stimulus: Initialize PC to 0x00000100 via reset sequence; rst_n=1; stall_i=0; flush_i=0; all redirect valids=0; instr_is_16b_i=1; reset_vector_i=0x00000100.
testcases.expected: Next pc_o and instr_addr_o advance to 0x00000102.

checks.condition: Cycle after sequential update from PC=0x00000100 with instr_is_16b_i=1
Cycle after sequential update from PC=0x00000100 with instr_is_16b_i=1

timing_contract.notes: Sequential PC update is registered.

testcases.stimulus: Initialize PC to 0x00000100 via reset sequence; rst_n=1; stall_i=0; flush_i=0; all redirect valids=0; instr_is_16b_i=0; reset_vector_i=0x00000100.
testcases.expected: Next pc_o and instr_addr_o advance to 0x00000104.

checks.condition: Cycle after sequential update from PC=0x00000100 with instr_is_16b_i=0
Cycle after sequential update from PC=0x00000100 with instr_is_16b_i=0

timing_contract.notes: Sequential PC update is registered.

testcases.stimulus: rst_n=1; stall_i=0; flush_i=1 or 0 per implementation; trap_redirect_valid_i=0; mret_redirect_valid_i=0; branch_redirect_valid_i=1; branch_redirect_pc_i=0x00000200; instr_is_16b_i=0; other redirect PCs arbitrary.

timing_contract.notes: Redirect sampled after clock edge.

testcases.stimulus: rst_n=1; stall_i=0; trap_redirect_valid_i=0; mret_redirect_valid_i=1; mret_redirect_pc_i=0x00000300; branch_redirect_valid_i=1; branch_redirect_pc_i=0x00000200; flush_i arbitrary; instr_is_16b_i=0.
testcases.expected: pc_o and instr_addr_o take the MRET target 0x00000300, not branch target.

checks.condition: Cycle after simultaneous MRET and branch redirects
Cycle after simultaneous MRET and branch redirects
